news.title:
Quectel and Syrma SGS expand their partnership to locally manufacture IoT antennas in India.
This move boosts IoT supply chain, supports 'Make in India', and enhances deployment for key industries.
news.summary:
Quectel and Syrma SGS expand their partnership to locally manufacture IoT antennas in India.
This move boosts IoT supply chain, supports 'Make in India', and enhances deployment for key industries.

news.title:
Syrma SGS plans Rs 1,600 crore investment in Andhra Pradesh for PCB and component manufacturing under ECMS.
Syrma targets trial production by 2027, diversifies into camera modules, and prioritizes local sourcing to boost 'Make in India'.
news.summary:
Syrma SGS plans Rs 1,600 crore investment in Andhra Pradesh for PCB and component manufacturing under ECMS.
Syrma targets trial production by 2027, diversifies into camera modules, and prioritizes local sourcing to boost 'Make in India'.

news.title:
MeitY approved 75 ECMS applications, attracting Rs 61,671 crore investments surpassing its Rs 59,350 crore target.
Key investors include Dixon (Rs 1,100 crore) and Syrma (Rs 588 crore), enhancing India's electronic component capabilities.
news.summary:
MeitY approved 75 ECMS applications, attracting Rs 61,671 crore investments surpassing its Rs 59,350 crore target.
Key investors include Dixon (Rs 1,100 crore) and Syrma (Rs 588 crore), enhancing India's electronic component capabilities.

news.title:
Govt plans to recalibrate PLI 2.0 for IT hardware, focusing on AI servers and advanced components.
EMS players Syrma, Dixon, and Kaynes may benefit if policy aligns with industry expectations.
news.summary:
Govt plans to recalibrate PLI 2.0 for IT hardware, focusing on AI servers and advanced components.

news.title: Syrma SGS shares rise 5% after Investec upgrades rating to 'Buy'.
news.summary: Syrma SGS shares rise 5% after Investec upgrades rating to 'Buy'.

news.title:
Syrma SGS extends long stop date to April 15, 2026, for acquiring 49% stake in Ksolare Energy.
Joint venture with Premier Energies; prior updates were on Oct 23, 2025, and Jan 29, 2026.
news.summary:

news.title:
Syrma SGS & Dixon Tech reassess semiconductor plans post ISM 2.0 announcement.
ISM 2.0 aims to build upstream capabilities; EMS firms eye margin expansion.
news.summary:
Syrma SGS & Dixon Tech reassess semiconductor plans post ISM 2.0 announcement.

news.title:
India-US trade deal boosts policy certainty for electronics makers like Tata Electronics, Foxconn, Dixon, and Syrma SGS.
Apple plans to double iPhone production in India, targeting 50M+ units in 3 years, reinforcing India's export growth.
news.summary:

news.title: Morgan Stanley upgrades Syrma SGS to 'Overweight' and sets a target price of Rs 992.

news.title:
Syrma's Q3 earnings show improvement, turning cash flow positive.
Dixon faces challenges due to a slowdown in the mobile segment.
news.summary:
Syrma's Q3 earnings show improvement, turning cash flow positive.
Dixon faces challenges due to a slowdown in the mobile segment.

news.title:
Dixon and Syrma's strong earnings lifted EMS stocks like Amber, PG Electroplast, and Kaynes.
Dixon saw 10% margin expansion, while Syrma's revenue grew 45% YoY with a 12.6% EBITDA margin.
news.summary:
Dixon and Syrma's strong earnings lifted EMS stocks like Amber, PG Electroplast, and Kaynes.
Dixon saw 10% margin expansion, while Syrma's revenue grew 45% YoY with a 12.6% EBITDA margin.

news.title:
Syrma SGS shares surged 11%, marking their best January since listing.
The rise follows a strong Q3 performance by Syrma SGS Tech.
news.summary:
Syrma SGS shares surged 11%, marking their best January since listing.
The rise follows a strong Q3 performance by Syrma SGS Tech.

news.title:
Syrma SGS Tech Q3 EBITDA rose to 1.6B INR from 790.6M INR YoY.
EBITDA margin improved to 12.6% from 9.1% YoY.
news.summary:
Syrma SGS Tech Q3 EBITDA rose to 1.6B INR from 790.6M INR YoY.
EBITDA margin improved to 12.6% from 9.1% YoY.

news.title:
Syrma SGS Technology will host an earnings call on Jan 30, 2026, at 10:30 AM IST.
The call will discuss Q3FY26 results, featuring key management and a Q&A session.
news.summary:

news.title:
Laurus Labs' H1 FY26 revenue grew 33% YoY to ₹3,223 crore, with EBITDA margins at 25.4%, driving a 76% share price rise despite high P/E of 81x.
Jain Resource Recycling saw 27% revenue growth in H1 FY26, led by lead segment expansion, while Syrma SGS focuses on high-margin industrial/auto segments, boosting profitability.
news.summary:
Laurus Labs' H1 FY26 revenue grew 33% YoY to ₹3,223 crore, with EBITDA margins at 25.4%, driving a 76% share price rise despite high P/E of 81x.
Jain Resource Recycling saw 27% revenue growth in H1 FY26, led by lead segment expansion, while Syrma SGS focuses on high-margin industrial/auto segments, boosting profitability.

news.title:
India's electronics industry may reach $500B by 2030, matching IT contributions.
Electronics sector poised to grow 3x faster than IT in the next five years.
news.summary:
India's electronics industry may reach $500B by 2030, matching IT contributions.
Electronics sector poised to grow 3x faster than IT in the next five years.

news.title:
Dixon Tech likely to see flat Q3 with 4% PAT growth; Kaynes Tech expected to report 48% PAT rise on 45% revenue growth.
Syrma SGS, Amber, and PG Electroplast forecast robust YoY PAT growth; Avalon Tech upgraded to REDUCE on valuation roll forward.
news.summary:

news.title:
Syrma SGS Technology Ltd acquires 60% of Elcome Integrated Systems for INR 235 Cr.
Navicom Technology to become a wholly owned subsidiary of Elcome post acquisition.
news.summary:
Syrma SGS Technology Ltd acquires 60% of Elcome Integrated Systems for INR 235 Cr.

news.title:
Kaynes Technology faces financial scrutiny, impacting India's electronics sector, with concerns over accounting, cash flow, and capital for government incentives.
Kaynes' shares fell 30% since Dec 1; broader EMS sector including Dixon, Syrma, and Amber also saw declines amid investor concerns.
news.summary:
Kaynes Technology faces financial scrutiny, impacting India's electronics sector, with concerns over accounting, cash flow, and capital for government incentives.
Kaynes' shares fell 30% since Dec 1; broader EMS sector including Dixon, Syrma, and Amber also saw declines amid investor concerns.

news.title:
Syrma MD highlights the need for ₹10,000-15,000 crore in PCB investments to cut import reliance below 50%.
Syrma's ₹1,500 crore Andhra plant aims for trial production by Dec 2026; partnerships include MSI and Dynabook.
news.summary:
Syrma MD highlights the need for ₹10,000-15,000 crore in PCB investments to cut import reliance below 50%.
